skill.name: cloudformation-best-practices
skill.description: CloudFormation guidance for reviewing templates, improving stack design, and applying production-ready deployment practices.
skill.summary: Review and improve AWS CloudFormation templates for production use.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, network, C2, and weak crypto patterns, but review found they are Markdown and CloudFormation false positives. The skill contains guidance text and examples only, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown backticks and a YAML code fence as Ruby or shell execution. These lines document CloudFormation syntax and AWS commands, and no executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks appear inside Markdown instructions and examples, not executable source code. The only command text is guidance to validate templates or recover stacks.

low_findings.title: Private CIDR Example Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a hardcoded IP address, but the value is a private VPC CIDR example in a CloudFormation parameter default. It does not create an outbound connection or identify an external host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value 10.0.0.0/16 is an RFC1918 private network range used in a template example. There is no code that contacts that address.

low_findings.title: CloudFormation Text Misclassified as Blocker Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged C2 and weak crypto keywords, but review found only CloudFormation wording and template description text. No malware control logic or cryptographic implementation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is semantically about AWS CloudFormation and EC2 VPC resources. No suspicious behavior, obfuscation, or malicious instructions are present.

content.value_statement: CloudFormation templates can become hard to maintain, risky to deploy, and difficult to troubleshoot. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical reviews and production patterns.
actual_capabilities: Reviews CloudFormation YAML and JSON templates for maintainability and production readiness.
Suggests parameters, mappings, conditions, outputs, and cross-stack reference patterns.
Recommends retention policies for stateful AWS resources.
Guides nested stack and multi-environment template design.
Explains stack failure and drift troubleshooting workflows.
limitations: It does not deploy stacks or call AWS APIs by itself.
It does not replace AWS service documentation or account-specific policy review.
It cannot guarantee cost, quota, or regional availability without current environment details.
It focuses on raw CloudFormation, not Terraform or AWS CDK.

use_cases.title: Production Template Review
use_cases.description: Review a CloudFormation template before release and identify maintainability, rollback, and state retention issues.

use_cases.title: Stack Architecture Planning
use_cases.description: Design nested stacks, exports, and environment parameters for a service that spans multiple AWS accounts.

use_cases.title: Deployment Failure Triage
use_cases.description: Analyze stack update failures or drift symptoms and plan safe recovery steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Template Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CloudFormation template for basic best practices, readability, and production risks. Explain each recommendation in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Add Environment Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me adapt this CloudFormation template for dev, staging, and prod. Use parameters, mappings, conditions, and safe defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Nested Stacks
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose a nested stack structure for this infrastructure. Show which resources belong together and which outputs should be exported.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Stack Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this drift or rollback situation. Identify likely causes, safe recovery steps, and template changes that reduce future risk.

output_examples.input: A VPC template with hardcoded environment names and no outputs.
output: The review identifies hardcoded values that should become parameters.
It recommends exports for values needed by other stacks.
It notes validation commands and linting checks before deployment.

output_examples.input: A production database stack without retention settings.
output: The review flags missing deletion and replacement protection for stateful resources.
It recommends explicit retention policies and a safer update plan.
It explains the operational risk in direct language.

output_examples.input: A stack stuck during rollback after a failed update.
output: The response separates immediate recovery steps from root cause fixes.
It explains when to skip a failed resource during rollback.
It recommends follow-up template changes to prevent repeat failures.

best_practices: Validate templates with AWS tools and linters before deployment.
Use parameters, mappings, conditions, and pseudo parameters instead of hardcoded environment values.
Apply retention and replacement policies to resources that store important data.
anti_patterns: Do not place every resource in one large template when nested stacks would clarify ownership.
Do not hardcode account IDs, ARNs, or region-specific values unless there is a documented reason.
Do not update failed stacks without understanding rollback state and resource impact.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy CloudFormation stacks?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and troubleshooting steps, but it does not run AWS commands by itself.

faq.question: Does it support both YAML and JSON templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance can apply to both formats, although the skill recommends YAML for readability.

faq.question: Can it help with nested stacks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest stack boundaries, outputs, exports, and cross-stack reference patterns.

faq.question: Can it replace cfn-lint or cfn-nag?
faq.answer: No. It complements those tools by explaining architecture, maintainability, and operational concerns.

faq.question: Is this useful for Terraform or CDK projects?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. The skill is focused on raw AWS CloudFormation templates.

faq.question: What information should I provide for a review?
faq.answer: Provide the template, target environment, deployment goal, known failures, and any account or region constraints.
